introSection: Rollin' Thunder is a steel roller coaster located at Tropic Falls in Foley, Alabama, USA.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
Rollin' Thunder was announced along with Tropic Falls (then known as Park at OWA) at the 2016 IAAPA Expo in Orlando, Florida, USA.

The coaster officially opened on July 21, 2017.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: Rollin' Thunder is a clone of Thunderbolt at Luna Park in New York. The only difference is that this coaster is blue, while Thunderbolt is orange.

sections.title: Ride experience
sections.content: The car leaves the station, makes a left turn and climbs a vertical chain lift hill. Riders plunge downwards and enter a vertical loop. This is followed by a zero-g roll and an over-banked curve. The car flips through a dive loop and moves through some airtime hills. After the airtime hills, riders head into a corkscrew, followed by an another airtime hill. The car then hits the final brake run and heads back into the station.

fullText:
Rollin' Thunder is a steel roller coaster located at Tropic Falls in Foley, Alabama, USA.

Rollin' Thunder was announced along with Tropic Falls (then known as Park at OWA) at the 2016 IAAPA Expo in Orlando, Florida, USA.

The coaster officially opened on July 21, 2017.

Rollin' Thunder is a clone of Thunderbolt at Luna Park in New York. The only difference is that this coaster is blue, while Thunderbolt is orange.

The car leaves the station, makes a left turn and climbs a vertical chain lift hill. Riders plunge downwards and enter a vertical loop. This is followed by a zero-g roll and an over-banked curve. The car flips through a dive loop and moves through some airtime hills. After the airtime hills, riders head into a corkscrew, followed by an another airtime hill. The car then hits the final brake run and heads back into the station.
